paper.title: Minichromosome maintenance 4 plays a key role in protecting against acute kidney injury by regulating tubular epithelial cells survival and regeneration
authors: Minichromosome maintenance 4 plays a key role in protecting against acute kidney injury by regulating tubular epithelial cells survival and regeneration

entities.name: Minichromosome maintenance 4 plays a key role in protecting against acute kidney injury by regulating tubular epithelial cells survival and regeneration

evidence_entities.text: Acute kidney injury (AKI) is a global public health problem characterized by the sudden deterioration of kidney function, posing a high risk for chronic kidney disease
Proximal tubular epithelial cells (PTCs) are highly susceptible to various types of injury but possess remarkable regenerative potential
Minichromosome maintenance 4 (MCM4), part of the MCM2-7 complex, is crucial for initiating DNA synthesis
MCM4 is highly unregulated in renal tubules during AKI development
Proximal tubule-specific MCM4 knockdown aggravates cisplatin-induced kidney dysfunction and tubular injury
MCM4 deficiency inhibits cell proliferation and DNA synthesis, promotes G1 phase arrest and cell apoptosis in cisplatin-treated TECs
